attacking_style: Rarely initiates decisive attacks himself; more often positioned in breakaways or select groups on climbs. When does attack, struggles to sustain efforts in final kilometers, showing explosive power that cannot be capitalized on due to fatigue.
team_tactics: Team frequently burns domestiques early through high-tempo pacing on climbs, leaving him isolated when race explodes in final 6km. Often employed in breakaway-planting tactics (2019 TDF, 2017 TDF) rather than traditional lead-out protection.
terrain_preference: Performs best on sustained, steady gradients where team tempo can control. Exposed on explosive, late-race accelerations inside final 6km and on technical descents where positioning becomes critical.
vulnerability: Third-week fade pattern across multiple Grand Tours (2022 Vuelta, 2023 TDF). Isolated when team depth exhausted early; cannot respond to multi-pronged attacks from deeper teams. Early-climb strength rarely translates to finish-line results.
key_patterns: Team burns domestiques early via tempo, leaving him isolated in decisive final kilometers (4+ instances)
Looks strongest early on climbs but cracks before summit or finale (2017 TDF, 2022 Giro)
Third-week Grand Tour form collapse pattern documented across 2022 Vuelta and 2023 TDF
Cannot sustain created gaps despite showing explosive power, fatigue limits capitalizing (2022 Giro stage 19)
Podium-capable but not victory-capable: multiple 3rd-4th places when team invests maximum resources
Team employs breakaway-plant tactics utilizing him as tempo weapon rather than protected finisher
form_trajectory: negative: Clear decline through 2023 with early GC weaknesses on Jaizkibel and Marie Blanc, plus isolation issues when Bahrain depth exhausted. Late-career consistency at podium level (2022 Lombardia) but no longer top-tier GC threat.
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 17
